name: Livermorium
appearance: A highly radioactive metal, of which only a few atoms have ever been made.
uses: At present, it is only used in research.
naturalAbundance: Livermorium does not occur naturally. It is made by bombarding curium atoms with calcium. The most stable isotope has a half-life of about 53 milliseconds.
biologicalRole: It has no known biological role.
